title: Authorize every resource access, not just the route
description: Check that the authenticated principal owns or may act on the specific object on every data access, not only at login or route entry.
body: Use when a handler reads or mutates a record keyed by a client-supplied id. Do not assume route-level auth implies object-level rights, and avoid trusting ids from the client as proof of ownership. Actions: load the resource, verify the current principal's permission against that object (tenant, owner, role), and run the check server-side before returning or writing; record the authorization predicate next to the query. Validation signals include a test where user A is denied access to user B's id and an audit log of denied attempts. Risk boundary: a missing object-level check is a direct IDOR vulnerability; deny by default and escalate any access path that cannot express ownership.
